title: Pigmented lesion review with referral and photo documentation

transcript.speaker: GP
transcript.text: What has changed about the mole?

transcript.speaker: Patient
transcript.text: It is on my upper back. My partner says it has grown and the edge looks uneven over three months.

transcript.speaker: GP
transcript.text: Any bleeding, itching, personal or family melanoma history, or lots of sunburns?

transcript.speaker: Patient
transcript.text: It itches sometimes. No bleeding. Dad had melanoma. I had a lot of sunburns as a kid.

transcript.speaker: GP
transcript.text: On dermoscopy it is asymmetric with irregular pigment network. I am taking a clinical and dermoscopic photo and referring urgently for excision/skin specialist review.

transcript.speaker: GP
transcript.text: Please book within two weeks. Call us if you have not heard in a week. Return sooner for bleeding, rapid growth, or pain.

candidate_notes.note: Changing pigmented lesion upper back x3 months: growth, irregular edge, intermittent itch. No bleeding. Risk: father melanoma, childhood sunburns. Dermoscopy asymmetric with irregular pigment network. Clinical and dermoscopic photos taken. Urgent referral for excision/skin specialist review, book within 2 weeks; patient to call if no appointment in 1 week. Return sooner for bleeding, rapid growth, pain.
Mole on upper back itchy. No bleeding. Likely benign naevus. Monitor for change and review routinely.

S: Upper-back lesion growing, uneven edge over 3 months, itchy. Father had melanoma, childhood sunburns. O: dermoscopy asymmetric, irregular pigment network. A: suspicious pigmented lesion. P: urgent referral for excision/specialist review within 2 weeks.

Suspicious pigmented lesion: change/growth, irregular border, itch, family history melanoma, sunburn history, asymmetric irregular dermoscopy. Photos taken. Urgent excision/skin specialist referral; book within 2 weeks and call practice if no appointment within 1 week. Return sooner for bleeding, rapid growth, pain.

CDSS skin-lesion checklist: evolving lesion plus melanoma risk factors and concerning dermoscopy. Documented clinical and dermoscopic photos. Urgent referral for excision/specialist review within 2 weeks; closed-loop instruction to call if no appointment in 1 week. Safety-net: bleeding, rapid growth, pain.
